title: Simplifying an expression using index laws

steps.instruction: Apply the power of a power law $(x^m)^n = x^{mn}$ to the numerator's first factor.
Apply the multiplication law $x^m \times x^n = x^{m+n}$ to multiply the two powers in the numerator.
Apply the division law $\dfrac{x^m}{x^n} = x^{m-n}$ to divide by the denominator.
Identify and take out the highest common factor (HCF) of all three terms.
Factorise the trinomial $x^2 + x - 6$ by finding two numbers that multiply to $-6$ and add to $1$.
Write the factorised form of the trinomial.
Include the common factor to give the complete factorisation.

title: Using the discriminant to determine the nature of roots
problem: For the quadratic $y = 3x^2 - 6x + 4$, calculate the discriminant and determine how many $x$-intercepts the parabola has.

steps.instruction: Identify the values of $a$, $b$ and $c$ from $y = ax^2 + bx + c$.
Substitute into the discriminant formula $\Delta = b^2 - 4ac$.
Interpret the sign of $\Delta$. Since $\Delta < 0$, the quadratic has no real roots.
State the geometric interpretation: the number of $x$-intercepts equals the number of real roots.
steps.working: $$\text{No real roots} \implies \text{the parabola does not cross or touch the } x\text{-axis}$$
